author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

title: John 3.31

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that cometh from above is above all; he that is of the earth is earthly, and speaketh of the earth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A dreadful thing is the love of glory, dreadful and full of many evils; it is a thorn hard to be extracted, a wild beast untamable and many headed, arming itself against those that feed it; for as the worm eats through the wood from which it is born, as rust wastes the iron whence it comes forth, and moths the fleeces, so vainglory destroys the soul which nourishes it; and therefore we need great diligence to remove the passion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe here how long a charm John uses over the disciples affected by it, and can scarcely pacify them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he softens them with other words besides those already mentioned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what are these others?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that cometh from above,” he saith, “is above all; he that is of the earth, is earthly, and speaketh of the earth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since you make much ado with my testimony, and in this way say that I am more worthy of credit than He, you needs must know this, that it is impossible for One who cometh from heaven to have His credit strengthened by one that inhabiteth earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what means “above all,” what is the expression intended to show to us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That Christ hath need of nothing, but is Himself sufficient for Himself, and incomparably greater than all; of himself John speaks as being “of the earth, and speaking of the earth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not that he spake of his own mind, but as Christ said, “If I have told you of earthly things and ye believe not,” so calling Baptism, not because it was an “earthly thing,” but because He compared it when He spake with His own Ineffable Generation, so here John said that he spake “of earth,” comparing his own with Christ’s teaching.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the “speaking of earth” means nothing else than this, “My things are little and low and poor compared with His, and such as it was probable that an earthly nature would receive.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In Him ‘are hid all the treasures of wisdom.’” ( Col. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That he speaks not of human reasonings is plain from this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that is of the earth,” saith he, “is earthly.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet not all in him was earthly, but the higher parts were heavenly, for he had a soul, and was partaker of a Spirit which was not of earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then saith he that he is “earthly”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou not that he means only, “I am small and of no esteem, going on the ground and born in the earth; but Christ came to us from above.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Having by all these means quenched their passion, he afterwards speaks more openly of Christ; for before this it was useless to utter words which could never have gained a place in the understanding of his hearers: but when he hath pulled up the thorns, he then boldly casts in the seed, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that cometh from above is above all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what He hath heard He speaketh, and what He hath seen He testifieth; and no man receiveth His testimony.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Having uttered something great and sublime concerning Him, he again brings down his discourse to a humbler strain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the expression, “what He hath heard and seen,” is suited rather to a mere man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He knew He knew not from having learned it by sight, or from having heard it, but He included the whole in His Nature, having come forth perfect from the Bosom of His Father, and needing none to teach Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, “As the Father,” He saith, “knoweth Me, even so know I the Father.” ( c. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then means, “He speaketh that He hath heard, and testifieth that He hath seen”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since by these senses we gain correct knowledge of everything, and are deemed worthy of credit when we teach on matters which our eyes have embraced and our ears have taken in, as not in such cases inventing or speaking falsehoods, John desiring here to establish this point, said, “What He hath heard and seen”: that is, “nothing that cometh from Him is false, but all is true.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus we when we are making curious enquiry into anything, often ask, “Didst thou hear it?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Didst thou see it?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if this be proved, the testimony is indubitable, and so when Christ Himself saith, “As I hear, I judge” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 30 ); and, “What I have heard from My Father, that I speak” ( c. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 15 ); and, “We speak that We have seen” ( c. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 11 ); and whatsoever other sayings He uttereth of the kind, are uttered not that we might imagine that He saith what He doth being taught of any, (it were extreme folly to think this,) but in order that nothing of what is said may be suspected by the shameless Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For because they had not yet a right opinion concerning Him, He continually betakes Himself to His Father, and hence makes His sayings credible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And why wonderest thou if He betake Himself to the Father, when He often resorts to the Prophets and the Scriptures? as when He saith, “They are they that testify of Me.” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Shall we then say that He is inferior to the Prophets, because He draws testimonies from them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Away with the thought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is because of the infirmity of His hearers that He so orders His discourse, and saith that He spake what He spake having heard it from the Father, not because He needed a teacher, but that they might believe that nothing that He said was false.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: John’s meaning is of this kind: “I desire to hear what He saith, for He cometh from above, bringing thence those tidings which none but life knoweth rightly; for ‘what He hath seen and heard,’ is the expression of one who declareth this.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And no man receiveth His testimony.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet He had disciples, and many besides gave heed to His words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then saith John, “No man”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He saith “no man,” instead of “few men,” for had he meant “no man at all,” how could he have added,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that hath received His testimony, hath set to his seal that God is true.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here he touches his own disciples, as not being likely for a time to be firm believers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that they did not even after this believe in Him, is clear from what is said afterwards; for John even when dwelling in prison sent them thence to Christ, that he might the more bind them to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet even then they scarcely believed, to which Christ alluded when He said, “And blessed is he whosoever shall not be offended in Me.” ( Matt. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore now he said, “And no man receiveth His testimony,” to make sure his own disciples; all but saying, “Do not, because for a time few shall believe on Him, therefore deem that His words are false; for, ‘He speaketh that He hath seen.’”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Moreover he saith this to touch also the insensibility of the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A charge which the Evangelist at commencing brought against them, saying, “He came unto His own, and His own received Him not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is no reproach against Him, but an accusation of those who received Him not. ( c. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that hath received His testimony hath set to his seal that God is true.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here he terrifies them also by showing that he who believeth not on Him, disbelieveth not Him alone, but the Father also; wherefore he adds:

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He whom God hath sent speaketh the words of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since then He speaketh His words, he that believeth and he that believeth not, believeth or believeth not God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Hath set to His seal,” that is, “hath declared.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, to increase their dread, he saith, “that God is true;” thus showing, that no man could disbelieve Christ without making God who sent Him guilty of a falsehood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because, since He saith nothing save what is from the Father, but all that He saith is His, he that heareth not Him, heareth not Him that sent Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: See how by these words again he strikes them with fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As yet they thought it no great thing not to hearken to Christ; and therefore he held so great a danger above the heads of the unbelievers, that they might learn that they hearken not to God Himself, who hearken not to Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then he proceeds with the discourse, descending to the measure of their infirmity, and saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For God giveth not the Spirit by measure.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, as I said, he brings down his discourse to lower ground, varying it and making it suitable to be received by those who heard it then; otherwise he could not have raised them and increased their fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had he spoken anything great and sublime concerning Jesus Himself, they would not have believed, but might even have despised Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore he leads up all to the Father, speaking for a while of Christ as of a man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what is it that he saith, “God giveth not the Spirit by measure”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He would show that we all have received the operation of the Spirit, by measure, (for in this place he means by “Spirit” the operation of the Spirit, for this it is that is divided,) but that Christ hath all Its operation unmeasured and entire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if His operations be unmeasured, much more His Essence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou too that the Spirit is Infinite?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then can He who hath received all the operation of the Spirit, who knoweth the things of God, who saith, “We speak that We have heard, and testify that We have seen” ( c. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 11 ), be rightly suspected?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He saith nothing which is not “of God,” or which is not of “the Spirit.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And for a while he uttereth nothing concerning God the Word, but maketh all his doctrine credible by (reference to) the Father and the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that there is a God they knew, and that there is a Spirit they knew, (even though they held not a right opinion concerning Him,) but that there is a Son, they knew not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is for this reason that he ever has recourse to the Father and the Spirit, thence confirming his words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if any one should take no account of this reason, and examine his language by itself, it would fall very far short of the Dignity of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Christ was not therefore worthy of their faith, because He had the operation of the Spirit, (for He needeth not aid from thence,) but is Himself Self-sufficient; only for a while the Baptist speaks to the understanding of the simpler sort, desiring to raise them up by degrees from their low notions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this I say, that we may not carelessly pass by what is contained in the Scriptures, but may fully consider the object of the speaker, and the infirmity of the hearers, and many other points in them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For teachers do not say all as they themselves would wish, but generally as the state of their weak (hearers) requires.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore Paul saith, “I could not speak unto you as unto spiritual, but as unto carnal; I have fed you with milk, and not with meat.” ( 1 Cor. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He means, “I desired indeed to speak unto you as unto spiritual, but could not”; not because he was unable, but because they were not able so to hear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So too John desired to teach some great things to the disciples, but they could not yet bear to receive them, and therefore he dwells for the most part on that which is lowlier.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It behooves us therefore to explore all carefully.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the words of the Scriptures are our spiritual weapons; but if we know not how to fit those weapons and to arm our scholars rightly, they keep indeed their proper power, but cannot help those who receive them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For let us suppose there to be a strong corselet, and helm, and shield, and spear; and let one take this armor and put the corselet upon his feet, the helmet over his eyes instead of on his head, let him not put the shield before his breast, but perversely tie it to his legs: will he be able to gain any advantage from the armor? will he not rather be harmed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is plain to any one that he will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet not on account of the weakness of the weapons, but on account of the unskillfulness of the man who knows not how to use them well.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So with the Scriptures, if we confound their order; they will even so retain their proper force, yet will do us no good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Although I am always telling you this both in private and in public, I effect nothing, but see you all your time nailed to the things of this life, and not so much as dreaming of spiritual matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore our lives are careless, and we who strive for truth have but little power, and are become a laughing stock to Greeks and Jews and Heretics.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Had ye been careless in other matters, and exhibited in this place the same indifference as elsewhere, not even so could your doings have been defended; but now in matters of this life, every one of you, artisan and politician alike, is keener than a sword, while in necessary and spiritual things we are duller than any; making by-work business, and not deeming that which we ought to have esteemed more pressing than any business, to be by-work even.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Know ye not that the Scriptures were written not for the first of mankind alone, but for our sakes also?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hearest thou not Paul say, that “they are written for our admonition, upon whom the ends of the world are come; that we through patience and comfort of the Scriptures might have hope”? ( 1 Cor. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 11; Rom. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I know that I speak in vain, yet will I not cease to speak, for thus I shall clear myself before God, though there be none to hear me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He that speaketh to them that give heed hath this at least to cheer his speech, the persuasion of his hearers; but he that speaks continually and is not listened to, and yet ceaseth not to speak, may be worthy of greater honor than the other, because he fulfills the will of God, even though none give heed unto him, to the best of his power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Still, though our reward will be greater owing to your disobedience, we rather desire that it be diminished, and that your salvation be advanced, thinking that your being well approved of is a great reward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And we now say this not to make our discourse painful and burdensome to you, but to show to you the grief which we feel by reason of your indifference.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: God grant that we may be all of us delivered from this, that we may cling to spiritual zeal and obtain the blessings of heaven, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, with whom to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, for ever and ever.
